symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden prodrome-free syncope triggered instantaneously by a loud acoustic/startle stimulus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Auditory/startle-triggered syncope is the classic, near-specific trigger for LQT2.
An instantaneous startle collapse with no prodrome is atypical for a vasovagal faint.

symptoms.symptom_name: Convulsive syncope — brief stiffening/jerking with rapid full recovery, no prolonged post-ictal confusion, no tongue biting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Transient cerebral hypoperfusion from a self-terminating arrhythmia produces brief convulsive movements.
Brevity, absent aura, no prolonged post-ictal state and no tongue biting favour convulsive syncope over epilepsy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Family history of sudden unexplained death in a young relative (maternal aunt, age 25)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Young sudden unexplained death is a marker of a heritable arrhythmia syndrome and raises pre-test probability.
Also a heritable cause of young sudden cardiac death that the family history keeps in view.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of chest pain or palpitations before the collapse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No preceding chest pain makes an ischaemic trigger less likely, though it does not exclude a primary arrhythmia which is typically painless.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of a typical vasovagal prodrome (no nausea, warmth, sweating, or tunnel vision)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lack of the classic autonomic prodrome makes a benign reflex faint unlikely and unsafe to assume.
Abrupt, warning-free collapse is characteristic of an arrhythmic (cardiac) syncope.

symptoms.symptom_name: No regular medications and no QT-prolonging drugs (no macrolides, no SSRIs)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of QT-prolonging medications makes an acquired cause less likely and points toward a congenital channelopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prolonged corrected QT interval (QTc > 470 ms) on 12-lead ECG

investigation_details.expected_result: Prolonged QTc > 470 ms (Bazett) with a structurally normal heart
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The diagnosis is missed; the lethal arrhythmia substrate goes untreated with ongoing sudden-death risk.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A prolonged QTc with a structurally normal heart is the diagnostic hallmark — the pivotal test, unobtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Structurally normal heart with a normal resting rate and rhythm (examination/echo)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No murmur and a structurally normal heart make HCM less likely.
A structurally normal heart is fully compatible with a purely electrical channelopathy — reassurance about structure does not exclude LQTS.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Startle-triggered prodrome-free syncope + convulsive movements + family history of young sudden death + absent vasovagal prodrome together mark a lethal inherited channelopathy that mandates urgent ECG and monitoring.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The pivotal, first-line test — prolonged QTc confirms long QT syndrome and stratifies risk. Requires in-hospital ECG; telehealth cannot perform it.
Detects and allows immediate treatment of a recurrent Torsades de Pointes during the high-risk window.
Exclude acquired/reversible causes of QT prolongation and correct any abnormality.
Exclude structural causes of young sudden cardiac death such as hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Cardiac genetic testing and first-degree relative screening
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the channelopathy genotype and identifies at-risk family members; arranged by cardiology/genetics after the acute phase.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine CT brain / EEG as a primary work-up for 'seizure'
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Anchoring on epilepsy and ordering a neurological work-up first would miss the cardiac cause; the ECG comes first.
